task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided code meets all the requirements, but edge case handling could be improved.

results.got: raised TypeError("'int' object is not iterable")

trace: In this implementation, the function `median()` takes a sorted list as input, calculates its length (n), and checks for empty or invalid inputs. If either of these conditions is met, it returns `None`. Otherwise, it determines whether the list length is odd or even and returns the middle value when odd or the average of the two middle values when even. To handle an edge case, the function ensures that the input list is already sorted before calculating the median (to avoid sorting a possibly large list multiple times). Also, it checks for the condition `n % 4 != 0` to ensure that the length of the list is greater than 3 when it's even to avoid cases like [1, 2] where there isn't an actual middle value.
